name: BPC-157
subtitle: Body Protection Compound-157 | Pentadecapeptide
overview: Synthetic peptide derived from gastric juice protein, renowned for tissue repair, inflammation reduction, and GI protection capabilities. BPC-157 promotes blood vessel formation, enhances collagen synthesis, modulates growth factor expression, and protects against tissue damage through localized or systemic delivery.
mechanism: Promotes blood vessel formation (angiogenesis), enhances collagen synthesis, modulates growth factor expression including VEGF, and protects against tissue damage through localized or systemic delivery pathways.
keyBenefits: Accelerated tendon, ligament, muscle, and bone healing
Localized tissue repair with direct targeting

items.name: Tendon Healing
items.description: Accelerated tendon-to-bone healing with improved biomechanical properties; most effective with localized injection.

items.name: Muscle Recovery
items.description: Enhanced healing and reduced recovery following crush injuries and surgical procedures with direct tissue targeting.

items.name: Angiogenesis
items.description: Promotes blood vessel formation and improves vascularization through localized delivery.

items.name: Neuroprotection
items.description: Protective effects against neurotoxic agents and ischemic brain injury models.

items.name: Spinal Cord Injury
items.description: Improved functional recovery and reduced tissue damage in spinal cord injury studies.

items.name: Peripheral Nerves
items.description: Enhanced peripheral nerve regeneration and functional recovery after injury.

items.name: Ulcer Protection
items.description: Protective effects against gastric and duodenal ulcers through cytoprotective mechanisms.

items.name: Intestinal Repair
items.description: Acceleration of intestinal healing with reduced inflammatory markers in IBD models.

items.name: Mucosal Healing
items.description: Enhanced mucosal barrier function and accelerated epithelial regeneration.

deliveryMethods.overview: Most effective for localized healing when injected near injury site. Subcutaneous injection preferred for targeted tissue repair.

steps: Clean work area and hands thoroughly
Calculate required BAC water volume using calculator
Inject slowly down vial side (not directly onto powder)
Store in refrigerator, use within 28 days

keyBenefits: Inject close to injury site for localized healing
Rotate injection sites to prevent irritation

deliveryMethods.overview: Effective for gastric and intestinal conditions. Higher doses required due to lower bioavailability.
keyBenefits: Take on empty stomach 30 minutes before meals

interactions.notes: Complementary healing mechanisms; often used together in the Wolverine Stack.
BPC-157 increases GH receptor expression, amplifying growth hormone benefits.
BPC-157 upregulates GH receptors, enhancing effectiveness for tissue repair.
No known interactions; different mechanisms and receptor targets.
Safe combination; different pathways. Often combined in regenerative protocols.

timeline.effects: Reduced inflammation and pain at injection site
Improved healing rate and tissue repair

stopSigns: Persistent injection site reactions or infection signs
Unusual swelling or rash around injection area
contraindications: Blood thinners (consult doctor due to angiogenesis)

good: White, fluffy lyophilized powder (cake) filling vial bottom
Crystal clear solution after reconstitution with no particles
warning: Slight clumping that dissolves with gentle swirling (acceptable from shipping)
bad: Collapsed, melted, or powder stuck to vial sides (heat exposure)
Cloudy solution, particles, or precipitates after reconstitution

references.title: Gastric pentadecapeptide BPC 157 accelerates healing of transected rat Achilles tendon and in vitro stimulates tendocytes growth
references.keyFindings: Superior tendon healing with improved biomechanical properties (increased load of failure and Young's modulus), enhanced tendon-to-bone integration, and stimulated tendocyte growth in rat models.

references.title: Effective therapy of transected quadriceps muscle in rat: Gastric pentadecapeptide BPC 157
references.keyFindings: Accelerated quadriceps healing over 72-day period with improved biomechanics, walking recovery, muscle fiber regeneration with desmin positivity, and attenuated atrophy in rat models.

references.title: Stable gastric pentadecapeptide BPC 157 can improve the healing course of spinal cord injury and lead to functional recovery in rats
references.journal: Journal of Orthopaedic Surgery and Research
references.keyFindings: Significant functional recovery with improved motor function, resolved spasticity by day 15, and counteracted axonal necrosis, demyelination, and cyst formation across all stages of secondary injury.

references.title: BPC 157 Rescued NSAID-cytotoxicity Via Stabilizing Intestinal Permeability and Enhancing Cytoprotection
references.authors: Park, J.M., Lee, H.J., Sikiric, P., Hahm, K.B.
references.keyFindings: Cytoprotective effects against NSAID-induced gastric and intestinal damage by stabilizing intestinal permeability and protecting mucosal barrier function across multiple epithelia.

references.title: Therapeutic potential of pro-angiogenic BPC157 is associated with VEGFR2 activation and up-regulation
references.keyFindings: Pro-angiogenic effects via VEGFR2-Akt-eNOS signaling pathway activation, increased vessel density in vivo and in vitro, and accelerated blood flow recovery in ischemic rat hind limb model.

references.title: Preclinical safety evaluation of body protective compound-157, a potential drug for treating various wounds
references.keyFindings: Well tolerated in mice, rats, rabbits, and dogs with no serious toxicity in single-dose, repeated-dose, local tolerance, genetic, or embryo-fetal toxicity studies.

references.title: Effect of BPC-157 on Symptoms in Patients with Interstitial Cystitis: A Pilot Study
references.authors: Lee, E., Walker, C., Ayadi, B.
references.journal: Alternative Therapies in Health and Medicine
references.keyFindings: Pilot human trial in 12 women (ages 39-76) with interstitial cystitis showed BPC-157 as a potential treatment for this disabling condition with no effective existing therapies.

references.title: Safety of Intravenous Infusion of BPC157 in Humans: A Pilot Study
references.journal: Alternative Therapies in Health and Medicine
references.keyFindings: First human IV safety data: infusions of up to 20 mg BPC-157 in 2 healthy adults showed no adverse effects on cardiac, hepatic, renal, thyroid, or glucose biomarkers.

latestResearch.title: Emerging Use of BPC-157 in Orthopaedic Sports Medicine: A Systematic Review
latestResearch.summary: Systematic review of 36 studies (35 preclinical, 1 clinical) from 1993-2024 showing BPC-157 enhances GH receptor expression, promotes angiogenesis, and reduces inflammatory cytokines in musculoskeletal injuries.

latestResearch.title: Multifunctionality and Possible Medical Application of the BPC 157 Peptide-Literature and Patent Review
latestResearch.summary: Comprehensive review by Jozwiak et al. of biological activities, mechanisms, and therapeutic potential across multiple organ systems including tissue injury and inflammatory bowel disease.

latestResearch.title: Safety of Intravenous Infusion of BPC157 in Humans: A Pilot Study
latestResearch.source: Alternative Therapies in Health and Medicine
latestResearch.summary: First human IV safety study showing no adverse effects at doses up to 20 mg in healthy adults, establishing initial human safety profile.

latestResearch.title: Pharmacokinetics, Distribution, Metabolism, and Excretion of Body-Protective Compound 157
latestResearch.summary: IM bioavailability 14-19% in rats, 45-51% in dogs; half-life less than 30 minutes; rapidly metabolized into small peptide fragments.

quickStats.storage: Lyophilized: freezer long-term. Reconstituted: 2-8°C for 4-6 weeks
